Black Tuna Gang.description: The Black Tuna Gang was a Miami-based marijuana smuggling organization active in the 1970s, led by Robert Platshorn and Robert Meinster. The group never used the name themselves; it was coined by media based on their gold medallion emblem. They imported an estimated 500 tons of marijuana over 16 months using sophisticated methods, including monitoring police communications. The gang was dismantled by a joint FBI-DEA operation, with eight members convicted and sentenced to lengthy prison terms.

'Ndrina Iozzo.description: The Iozzo-Chiefari are a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta, based in Chiaravalle Centrale and Torre di Ruggiero, Calabria, Italy. They are part of the Locale of Serra San Bruno. The group is involved in drug trafficking (marijuana and cocaine), illegal weapons possession, and illicit activities in construction, earthmoving, wholesale timber, and subcontracting. They have been targeted in multiple police operations, including 'Ortrhus' in 2019 and 'Anteo' in 2021.

Albanian mafia.description: The Albanian mafia is a term for various criminal organizations based in Albania, Kosovo, and other Albanian-majority territories, or composed of ethnic Albanians. Known for ferocity and violence, they are active in the United States and European Union, involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and human trafficking. Their roots lie in traditional family clans (Fis) and the Kanun code, and they expanded internationally after the fall of communism and the Kosovo War.

Los Ardillos.description: Los Ardillos is a Mexican criminal group based in the La Montaña region of Guerrero, founded around 2000 by Celso Ortega Rosas and his sons. It is responsible for drug trafficking, extortion, kidnapping, and violence, particularly against indigenous communities. The group has been allied with the Beltrán Leyva Cartel and later Guerreros Unidos, and has been in conflict with the Cártel de los Rojos and other groups. Despite the arrest and killing of its founder, the group remains active as of the present.

Bacchus Motorcycle Club.description: Canadian outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1972 in Albert County, New Brunswick. The club has been involved in drug trafficking and has expanded its influence through alliances and patch-overs.

'Ndrina Fiarè.description: The 'Ndrina Fiarè is a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in San Gregorio d'Ippona, province of Vibo Valentia, Italy. It was judicially recognized only in 2013. The clan is allied with the powerful Mancuso family and has been involved in drug trafficking, extortion, usury, money laundering, and political infiltration. Its leader Saverio Razionale moved to Rome in 2005, replicating the criminal network there.

American Mafia.description: The American Mafia is a highly organized Italian American criminal society that emerged in the late 19th century in impoverished Italian immigrant neighborhoods in New York City and other major U.S. cities. It is structured into families and engages in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, extortion, drug trafficking, and other illegal activities. The organization cooperates with Italian organized crime groups such as the Sicilian Mafia, Camorra, and 'Ndrangheta. It remains active primarily in the Northeastern and Midwestern United States, with some operations in Canada.

Los Negros.description: Los Negros was the main armed wing of the Beltrán Leyva Cartel in Mexico, formed in 2003 by Marcos Arturo Beltrán Leyva, Alfredo Beltrán Leyva, and Edgar Valdez Villarreal. Initially supporting the Sinaloa Cartel against the Gulf Cartel and Los Zetas, it later allied with Los Zetas after a split with Sinaloa. The group was led by Edgar Valdez Villarreal and involved in drug trafficking, murder, and extortion across multiple Mexican states. Following Valdez's arrest in 2010, the group fragmented into smaller independent cells.

Kyodo-kai.description: The Kyodo-kai is a yakuza group based in Hiroshima, Japan, formed in January 1969 by Kokichi Morita after the disbanding of the Takahashi-gumi. It is a designated yakuza group with an estimated 60 active members and is the second largest in the Chugoku region. The group has been involved in an anti-Yamaguchi federation called the Gosha-kai since 1996.

Latin Kings.description: Hispanic street gang founded in Chicago in 1954 by Puerto Ricans and other Latin American immigrants. Initially a mutual aid group, it evolved into a criminal organization involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and murder. The gang has two main factions: King Motherland Chicago and Bloodline, with an estimated 20,000-35,000 members in the KMC faction. It operates across the United States and has international presence in Canada, Ecuador, Italy, and Spain.

Comanchero Motorcycle Club.description: The Comanchero Motorcycle Club is an outlaw motorcycle gang based in Australia, with chapters in Southeast Asia and New Zealand. Founded in 1968 by William George 'Jock' Ross in Sydney, the club is known for its violent history, including the 1984 Milperra massacre. It is involved in drug trafficking and other criminal activities, and remains active with over 1,000 members as of 2019.

edges.evidence_quote: One of the major events in Australian motorcycle gang criminal history was what became known as the Milperra Massacre in 1984, where a fight between two gangs, the Comancheros and the Bandidos in Milperra in the South of Sydney, turned into a gun battle that claimed seven lives - six gang members and a civilian.
